description: Documentation template library for the Erwin OS Planner agent. Each template defines the standard structure, audience, and expected characteristics for a document type.

templates.name: Technical Specification
templates.description: Detailed technical specification for a system, feature, or integration. Defines requirements, architecture, interfaces, and acceptance criteria.

sections.title: Overview
sections.description: High-level summary of what is being specified, the problem it solves, and the scope of this document.

sections.title: Background & Context
sections.description: Business context, prior decisions, and the motivation driving this specification.

sections.title: Requirements
sections.description: Functional and non-functional requirements, clearly numbered. Include constraints and assumptions.

sections.title: System Design
sections.description: Architecture overview, component diagram, data flow, and key technical decisions with rationale.

sections.title: API / Interface Contracts
sections.description: Endpoints, schemas, input/output formats, error codes, and versioning strategy.

sections.title: Data Model
sections.description: Entity definitions, relationships, storage considerations, and migration notes.

sections.title: Security Considerations
sections.description: Authentication, authorization, data protection, and threat model notes.

sections.title: Testing Strategy
sections.description: Unit, integration, and E2E testing approach. Key test scenarios.

sections.title: Rollout Plan
sections.description: Deployment phases, feature flags, rollback procedures, and monitoring.

sections.title: Open Questions
sections.description: Unresolved decisions, pending inputs, and areas needing further discussion.

sections.title: References
sections.description: Links to related specs, ADRs, tickets, and external documentation.

templates.name: Standard Operating Procedure
templates.description: Step-by-step operational procedure for a recurring task or process. Must be unambiguous and executable by the target operator.
templates.audience: Operations staff, support engineers, or designated process owners

sections.title: Purpose
sections.description: Why this SOP exists, what process it governs, and when it should be followed.

sections.title: Scope
sections.description: What is covered and not covered by this procedure. Boundary conditions.

sections.title: Prerequisites
sections.description: Required access, tools, permissions, and knowledge before starting.

sections.title: Procedure Steps
sections.description: Numbered step-by-step instructions. Each step includes action, expected outcome, and what to do if the outcome is unexpected.

sections.title: Verification
sections.description: How to confirm the procedure was completed successfully.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.description: Common issues, their symptoms, and resolution steps.

sections.title: Rollback / Recovery
sections.description: How to undo the procedure if something goes wrong.

sections.title: Revision History
sections.description: Version log with date, author, and summary of changes.

templates.name: Architecture Decision Note
templates.description: Record of a significant architecture or design decision, including context, options considered, decision made, and consequences.
templates.audience: Engineering team, architects, and future maintainers

sections.title: Title & Status
sections.description: Decision title and current status (proposed, accepted, deprecated, superseded).

sections.title: Context
sections.description: The situation, forces, and constraints that created the need for this decision.

sections.title: Decision
sections.description: The decision that was made, stated clearly and concisely.

sections.title: Options Considered
sections.description: All alternatives that were evaluated, with pros and cons for each.

sections.title: Consequences
sections.description: Positive, negative, and neutral outcomes expected from this decision.

sections.title: Related Decisions
sections.description: Links to related ADRs or decisions that interact with this one.

templates.name: Workflow Documentation
templates.description: Description of a multi-step business or technical workflow, including actors, triggers, steps, decision points, and outcomes.
templates.audience: Team members involved in the workflow, managers, and process improvement leads

sections.title: Workflow Overview
sections.description: Summary of the workflow, its purpose, trigger conditions, and expected outcomes.

sections.title: Actors & Roles
sections.description: Who participates in the workflow and their responsibilities at each stage.

sections.title: Trigger Conditions
sections.description: What initiates the workflow. May include multiple entry points.

sections.title: Process Steps
sections.description: Sequential steps with decision points, branches, and handoffs clearly marked.

sections.title: Tools & Systems
sections.description: Which tools, platforms, or systems are used at each step.

sections.title: Exceptions & Edge Cases
sections.description: What happens when the normal flow breaks. Escalation paths.

sections.title: Metrics & SLAs
sections.description: Performance targets, time limits, and quality metrics for the workflow.

templates.name: Release Note
templates.description: Communication of what changed in a release — new features, improvements, fixes, and known issues.
templates.audience: End users, customers, internal stakeholders, and support teams

sections.title: Release Summary
sections.description: Version number, date, and one-paragraph summary of the release.

sections.title: New Features
sections.description: New capabilities added in this release, with brief descriptions.

sections.title: Improvements
sections.description: Enhancements to existing features.

sections.title: Bug Fixes
sections.description: Issues resolved, with ticket references where available.

sections.title: Breaking Changes
sections.description: Changes that require action from users. Migration guidance included.

sections.title: Known Issues
sections.description: Issues discovered but not yet resolved. Workarounds if available.

sections.title: Upgrade Instructions
sections.description: Steps to upgrade from the previous version.

templates.name: User Guide
templates.description: Comprehensive guide for end users on how to use a product, feature, or system. Focuses on tasks and outcomes rather than technical internals.
templates.audience: End users — varying technical levels depending on the product

sections.title: Introduction
sections.description: What this guide covers, who it's for, and what the reader will be able to do after reading.

sections.title: Getting Started
sections.description: First-time setup, account creation, installation, or onboarding steps.

sections.title: Core Features
sections.description: Detailed walkthrough of primary features with step-by-step instructions.

sections.title: Advanced Usage
sections.description: Power-user features, customization, integrations, and advanced configurations.

sections.title: FAQ
sections.description: Common questions and answers based on support history and user feedback.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.description: Common problems and their solutions.

sections.title: Glossary
sections.description: Definitions of key terms used in the guide.

templates.name: API Documentation
templates.description: Reference documentation for an API, covering endpoints, authentication, request/response formats, errors, and usage examples.

sections.title: Overview
sections.description: What the API does, base URL, versioning scheme, and authentication method.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.description: How to authenticate. Token types, scopes, and example auth flows.

sections.title: Endpoints
sections.description: Each endpoint with method, path, parameters, request body, response schema, and status codes.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.description: Error response format, common error codes, and troubleshooting guidance.

sections.title: Rate Limits
sections.description: Request limits, throttling behavior, and how to handle 429 responses.

sections.title: Webhooks
sections.description: Event types, payload formats, delivery guarantees, and retry logic.

sections.title: Code Examples
sections.description: Working examples in common languages (Python, JavaScript, cURL).

sections.title: Changelog
sections.description: API version history and breaking changes.

templates.name: Meeting Summary
templates.description: Structured record of a meeting including decisions made, action items, and key discussion points.
templates.audience: Meeting attendees and stakeholders who need visibility into decisions

sections.title: Meeting Details
sections.description: Date, time, attendees, facilitator, and purpose of the meeting.

sections.title: Agenda
sections.description: Topics that were planned for discussion.

sections.title: Key Discussion Points
sections.description: Summary of significant discussions, arguments, and context.

sections.title: Decisions Made
sections.description: Explicit list of decisions with rationale and who made them.

sections.title: Action Items
sections.description: Tasks assigned with owner, deadline, and description.

sections.title: Open Items
sections.description: Topics deferred or needing follow-up.

templates.name: QA / UAT Summary
templates.description: Summary of quality assurance or user acceptance testing results, including test coverage, pass/fail rates, defects found, and release readiness assessment.
templates.audience: QA leads, product managers, and release decision-makers

sections.title: Test Summary
sections.description: What was tested, test environment, duration, and overall pass/fail rate.

sections.title: Scope & Coverage
sections.description: Features and areas covered by testing. Explicitly note any untested areas.

sections.title: Results Breakdown
sections.description: Detailed results by feature area or test category. Include pass/fail/blocked counts.

sections.title: Defects Found
sections.description: List of defects with severity, description, reproduction steps, and current status.

sections.title: Risk Assessment
sections.description: Residual risks from untested areas, known defects, or environmental limitations.

sections.title: Release Recommendation
sections.description: Go/no-go recommendation with supporting rationale.

sections.title: Appendix: Test Cases
sections.description: Detailed test case list or link to test management system.

templates.name: Implementation Note
templates.description: Record of how something was implemented, capturing technical decisions, configurations, caveats, and post-implementation observations.
templates.audience: Engineering team, on-call engineers, and future maintainers

sections.title: Summary
sections.description: What was implemented, when, and by whom. Link to the originating ticket or spec.

sections.title: Approach
sections.description: Technical approach taken, including alternatives considered and why this approach was chosen.

sections.title: Configuration & Setup
sections.description: Environment variables, feature flags, infrastructure changes, and deployment configurations.

sections.title: Caveats & Gotchas
sections.description: Non-obvious behaviors, edge cases, workarounds, and technical debt introduced.

sections.title: Monitoring & Observability
sections.description: Dashboards, alerts, log queries, and metrics to watch.

sections.title: Rollback Procedure
sections.description: How to undo this implementation if problems arise.

sections.title: Follow-Up Items
sections.description: Remaining work, tech debt tickets, and future improvements.

templates.name: Support Handover
templates.description: Handover document for transferring support responsibility for a system, client, or domain to a new team or individual.
templates.audience: Incoming support team or individual taking over responsibilities

sections.title: Handover Summary
sections.description: What is being handed over, effective date, and parties involved.

sections.title: System / Client Overview
sections.description: High-level description of the system or client, their importance, and key contacts.

sections.title: Current State
sections.description: Active issues, ongoing projects, pending decisions, and known risks.

sections.title: Access & Credentials
sections.description: Required access to systems, tools, and communication channels. How to obtain credentials.

sections.title: Common Issues & Runbooks
sections.description: Frequently encountered problems and their resolution procedures.

sections.title: Escalation Paths
sections.description: Who to contact for different types of issues. SLA expectations.

sections.title: Key Documentation
sections.description: Links to existing documentation, wikis, runbooks, and architecture diagrams.

sections.title: Transition Timeline
sections.description: Shadow period, knowledge transfer sessions, and full handover date.
